REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ELDER ABUSE. NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXIST, AND DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO A JUDGMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE "RIGHT OF QUIET ENJOYMENT" IS "REAL OR PERSONAL PROPERTY" OF AN ELDER WITHIN THE MEANING OF WIC 15610.30 AND FAILED TO PROVIDE ANY EVIDENCE THAT THE DEFENDANT TOOK PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT FOR QUIET ENJOYMENT FOR WRONGFUL USE OR WITH INTENT TO DEFRAUD. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TENANT HARASSMENT IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE "BAD FAITH" ELEMENT OF SFRO 37.10B EXIST. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING ARISING OUT OF RENT INCREASE IS DENIED. PURSUANT TO THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING EACH PARTY TO THE CONTRACT IS OBLIGATED NOT TO ENGAGE IN CONDUCT FOR THE PURPOSE OF DENYING TO THE OTHER THE BENEFITS OF THE CONTRACT. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXIST AS TO THE PURPOSE OF DEFENDANTS' "RENT INCREASE" CONDUCT. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF RAISES AN UNPLED THEORY OF LIABILITY IN OPPOSITION RE: RENT BOARD POST-HEARING BRIEFS. THE COURT DOES NOT ADDRESS THIS THEORY AS THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS FRAMED BY THE PLEADINGS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP SECTION 437C(G) AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE CLAIMS, AS ALLEGED, ARISE OUT OF THE RENT INCREASE (NOT SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF RENT INCREASE) AND FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE/CONFIRM PLAINTIFF'S WHEREABOUTS, NEITHER IS A PROTECTED ACTIVITY WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 426.15. REFERENCES TO THE SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF RENT INCREASE AND THE RENT BOARD PROCEEDING DO NOT FORM THE BASIS OF LIABILITY FOR ANY OF THE CLAIMS. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-15-2024 Continued To Nov-18-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication [Ccp 437c]
Off Calendar May-21-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Andrew Steinfeld; 415-816-4197; appearing as counsel for plaintiff Herrick Carver.
Bryan Ceglio; Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP; 925-357-3456; appearing as counsel for defendants HVH Hayes Street LLC and ICON Real Estate Inc.

Defendants' counsel contests the court's tentative decision as it pertains to the Breach of Contract Cause of Action. The court rules on Plaintiff's Evidentiary Objections regarding McKinstry Declaration. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to the cause of action for elder abuse. No triable issues of fact exist, and Defendant is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. Plaintiff failed to cite any authority for the proposition that the "right of quiet enjoyment" is "real or personal property" of an elder within the meaning of WIC 15610.30 and failed to provide any evidence that the defendant took Plaintiff's right for quiet enjoyment for wrongful use or with intent to defraud. Motion for Summary Adjudication of the cause of action for tenant harassment is DENIED. Triable issues of fact as to the "bad faith" element of SFRO 37.10B exist. Motion for summary adjudication of the cause of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing arising out of rent increase is DENIED. Pursuant to the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing each party to the contract is obligated not to engage in conduct for the purpose of denying to the other the benefits of the contract. Triable issues of fact exist as to the purpose of defendants' "rent increase" conduct. The Court notes that Plaintiff raises an unpled theory of liability in opposition re: Rent Board post-hearing briefs. The Court does not address this theory as the Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is framed by the pleadings.

Prevailing party to prepare order in accordance with CCP Section 437c(g) and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar May-21-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Bryan Ceglio; Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP; 925-357-3456; appearing as counsel for defendants HVH Hayes Street LLC and ICON Real Estate Inc.

PLAINTIFF HERRICK CARVER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issues of fact as to the "bad faith" element of SFRO 37.10B exist.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2023 Continued To Oct-18-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES
Andrew Steinfeld (415)816-4197 appearing for plaintiff Herrick Carver.
Nils Rosenquest (415)292-0980 appearing for defendant HVH Hayes Street, LLC
Reporter: Tamara Willat; csr# 4609; (415)272-1753; tamarawillat @sbcglobal. net

Hearing held (See reporter's transcript for full details). After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

is DENIED. The claims, as alleged, arise out of the rent increase (not service of the notice of rent increase) and failure to investigate/confirm plaintiff's whereabouts, neither is a protected activity within the meaning of CCP 426.15. References to the service of the Notice of Rent Increase and the Rent Board proceeding do not form the basis of liability for any of the claims. Prevailing party to prepare order.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala; Reported: Tamara Willat; csr# 4609; (415)272-1753; tamarawillat @sbcglobal. net
